course_description: This course presents students with the concepts and methodologies of occupational hygiene, including the anticipation, recognition, evaluation, communication and control of environmental stressors in, or arising from, the workplace that may result in injury, illness, impairment, or affect the well-being of workers and members of the community. Through a range of lectures, tutorials, practicals and field visits, students will develop a high level understanding of the techniques for measuring and monitoring exposure to hazardous chemicals in the workplace and how exposure information can be used to assess risk. As part of this learning, students will develop skills needed to present the results of atmospheric monitoring in a form useful for health risk assessment purposes. The course will present the principles of control of occupational health hazards, including chemical, biological and physical hazards, and will require students to critically appraise these strategies. There will be a focus on three major types of occupational hygiene hazards: particulates (dusts, fumes and fibres); metals; and gases and vapours. Toxicological principles relevant to developing an understanding of workplace health hazards will also be reviewed such as dose response relationships, types of toxic effects and the elimination and detoxification of chemicals and biological agents.

The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
This course will be delivered over 13 weeks of face-to-face classes, including lectures, workshops, and practicals.
course_aims: The course aims to introduce students to the broad principles of occupational hygiene, including anticipation, recognition, evaluation and control of workplace health hazards. Focusing on the techniques for measuring, monitoring, assessing and controlling airborne contaminants.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate an understanding of and an ability to critically apply occupational hygiene principles to the anticipation, recognition, evaluation, communication and control of workplace health hazards.
Review and apply key toxicology principles, exposure standards and basic statistics to the evaluation of complex occupational health and hygiene risks.
Demonstrate an understanding of and an ability to apply the principles of the hierarchy of control to the selection of control measures for a range of complex occupational health hazards.
Select appropriate equipment to measure specific airborne contaminants and devise suitable sampling strategies.
Plan, conduct and prepare a scientific report on a complex survey of a workplace atmospheric contaminant in a manner useful for health risk assessment purposes.
Apply occupational hygiene principles to the completion of hazardous chemical risk assessments.

assessment_details.title: Problem solution
assessment_details.other_conditions: Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Students will be presented with a number of typical occupational hygiene issues and asked to prepare a response based on the scenario provided. The problem will require students to further develop and apply their knowledge and understanding of hazardous chemical monitoring and control. The assignment has a maximum word length of 2500 words. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Online submission by Turnitin only by the due date and time. Refer to Blackboard for the submission link. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets are required. Submission via email is not accepted.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
You are required to submit assessable items on time. If you fail to meet the submission deadline for any assessment item, then 10% of the maximum possible mark for the assessment item (the assessment ‘marked from’ value) will be deducted as a late penalty for every day (or part day) late after the due date.
For example, if you submit your assignment 1 hour late, you will be penalised 10%; if your assignment is 24.5 hours late, you will be penalised 20% (because it is late by one 24-hour period plus part of another 24-hour period).

assessment_details.title: Practical Report
assessment_details.other_conditions: Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Students will be required to prepare a scientific report based on the completion of a workplace occupational hygiene survey. The report is to be prepared in the style of a hygiene consultant's report with the aim of providing useful information for the purpose of completing a health risk assessment. The assignment has a maximum word length of 3000. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. assessment_details.title: Practical Assessment
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: 15 minutes in person practical assessment. The practical examination will require all students to demonstrate their understanding of the application of various instrumentation and equipment used for assessing occupational hygiene hazards. Each student will be assessed individually. Students will complete their practical assessment in person. Please refer to Blackboard for your scheduled presentation time and location. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
Students will be scheduled to deliver their assessment. You may only be eligible for alternative arrangement if exceptional circumstances arise on the day of the scheduled presentation. Please contact your course coordinator with supporting documentation of the exceptional circumstances that prevented you from attending for consideration of an alternate arrangement.

learning_resources.library_resources: Library resources are available on the UQ Library website .

learning_activities.topic: Introduction to occupational hygiene An introduction to occupational hygiene and its history, including an introduction to performing hazardous chemical risk assessments. Learning outcomes: L01, L02

Hazardous chemical qualitative risk assessments A practical class to learn how to complete a qualitative hazardous chemical risk assessment, including the control banding approach. Learning outcomes: L01, L02

Workplace Exposure Limits (WELs) Theory An overview of workplace exposure standards including a brief history, development of exposure standards, application, definitions, terminology, units, accessing exposure standards with examples, limitations, and potential problems (e.g. extended work schedule and workload considerations), etc. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L05, L06
Workplace Exposure LImits (WELs) Application Exposure standards practical class. Learning outcomes: L02, L05, L06

Occupational hygiene process A detailed presentation of the occupational hygiene process, including an overview of monitoring strategies, such as personal, workplace and biological. Learning outcomes: L01, L04, L05
Health Risk Assessment Tutorial exploring current approaches to health risk assessment for hazardous chemicals, including modelling approaches. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L06

Control of hazardous chemical exposures 1 Introduction to the hierarchy of controls, including the underlying principles and practical application. Identifying effective control strategies, adopting the principles of reasonably practicable. Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L06
Control of hazardous chemical exposures 2 Practical exercises on the principles of control Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L06
Ventilation 1 The use of ventilation systems for the control of hazardous chemical exposures, including their design and assessment - lecture 1 Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L06
Ventilation 2 The use of ventilation systems for the control of hazardous chemical exposures, including their design and assessment - lecture 2 Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L06
Respiratory Protective Equipment Practical Invited guest demonstrates the selection, fitting, care and use of respiratory protective equipment. Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L06

Ventilation Practical Practical exercises to demonstrate the use of ventilation to control airborne contaminants and the monitoring of the effectiveness of ventilation systems. Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L06
Aerosols Lecture Aerosols in the workplace: what are they? Development of workplace aerosol monitoring. Monitoring principles for aerosols. Learning outcomes: L01, L04, L05
Aerosols Practical Demonstration and practical class on aerosol sampling techniques. Learning outcomes: L01, L04, L05

Statistics Lecture and Tutorial The lecture will provide an introduction to sampling statistics, including the use of statistics in the analysis and interpretation of hygiene monitoring data. Learning outcomes: L01, L05
Report writing, communication and ethics Hygiene report writing, communication, and ethics tutorial Learning outcomes: L01, L05, L06

Fibrous Dusts - Asbestos and SMF Fibrous Dusts - Asbestos and SMF. What is asbestos, and what are the health effects? Sources and types of asbestos. Asbestos exposure in the workplace: past and present. Air sampling for asbestos. Synthetic mineral fibres, types and health effects. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05

Asbestos and SMF Practical Asbestos practical demonstration and exercises on asbestos sampling, fibre counting and identification, including calibration of pumps. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05

Gases and Vapours Lecture Types of gases and vapours, occupational health aspects of gases and vapours, monitoring of gases and vapours and commonly occurring gases and vapours. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05
Metals Practical Practical class on metals exposure monitoring. Learning outcomes: L01, L04, L05
Direct Reading Instruments for Gases and Vapours Demonstration and practical exercises using reading instruments to monitor for gases and vapours. Learning outcomes: L01, L04, L05
Gas and vapour sampling practical Demonstration and practical exercises using tubes and badges and direct-reading instruments to monitor for gases and vapours. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05
Dermal Exposure and Gloves Lecture on dermal exposure and PPE selection and use for managing dermal exposure. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L06
Dermal Practical Practical class on PPE, including gloves for dermal protection. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L06
Case Studies Case study tutorial and review for exam Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
Review for Practical Exam Practical class to prepare for the practical exam. Learning outcomes: L04
